title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 1
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: The student union at your college has asked you to write a report on students' clothing consumption habits and their awareness of sustainability issues in fashion. Your report should outline current habits, assess levels of awareness, and recommend ways the college could encourage more sustainable choices.
tasks.modelAnswer:
STUDENT CLOTHING HABITS AND SUSTAINABILITY AWARENESS: A REPORT

Introduction
This report examines clothing consumption habits among students at Heronfield College, evaluates their understanding of sustainability issues in fashion, and proposes initiatives to foster more responsible consumer behaviour.

Current Habits
A survey of 150 students revealed that the majority purchase new clothing at least twice a month, with fast fashion retailers cited as the primary source by 78% of respondents. Second-hand shopping is practised by only 22% of students, and fewer than one in ten reported ever repairing damaged garments rather than replacing them.

Awareness Levels
While most students expressed general concern about climate change, awareness of the specific environmental impact of fast fashion was limited. Only 34% were aware that the textile industry is one of the world's largest polluters, and fewer than half had encountered the term 'microplastic pollution' in the context of clothing.

Recommendations
The college should introduce a termly clothing swap event, enabling students to exchange garments without financial outlay. An educational campaign, delivered through social media and induction sessions, should highlight the environmental and social costs of fast fashion. Additionally, partnering with a local textile repair workshop to offer free mending sessions would provide a practical alternative to disposal.

Conclusion
By combining education with accessible sustainable alternatives, the college can meaningfully influence student attitudes and behaviour, contributing to a broader cultural shift away from disposable fashion.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt: You recently attended a local clothing swap event organised by a sustainability group and found it very well run. Write an email to the organiser, explaining what you found most effective about the event, suggesting one improvement, and proposing a potential collaboration with your college to run a similar event.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Subject: Feedback and Collaboration Proposal – Clothing Swap Event

Dear Ms Fernandez,

I am writing to congratulate you on last Saturday's clothing swap event at the Meridian Community Centre, which I attended with several friends. It was an exceptionally well-organised occasion, and I wanted to share some thoughts.

What struck me most was the welcoming atmosphere your team created. The clear labelling system for garment sizes and conditions made browsing straightforward, and the presence of knowledgeable volunteers ensured that participants felt guided rather than overwhelmed. The brief talk at the start, which contextualised the environmental rationale behind clothing swaps, was a particularly inspired touch — it gave the event genuine educational weight.

If I were to suggest one improvement, it would be to introduce a dedicated section for professional or formal wear. Many attendees I spoke with mentioned a lack of workwear options, and this could attract a wider demographic, including recent graduates entering the workforce.

I am also writing with a proposal. I represent the student sustainability society at Aldgate College, and we would be very keen to co-host a clothing swap event on campus next semester. We have access to a large common room, an established social media following among students, and a motivated committee of volunteers. A partnership would allow us to combine your expertise with our campus network to reach a broader audience.

I would welcome the opportunity to discuss this further at your convenience.

Kind regards,
Jamie Okafor
Sustainability Society, Aldgate College

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: An environmental magazine is inviting readers to submit reviews of documentaries or books about the fashion industry and sustainability. Write a review of a documentary or book you have seen or read, describing its content, evaluating its effectiveness in communicating its message, and recommending it to others.
tasks.modelAnswer:
STITCHED UP: A REVIEW OF 'THE TRUE COST'

'The True Cost' is a 2015 documentary directed by Andrew Morgan that investigates the global fast fashion industry, tracing the journey of a garment from cotton field to shop floor. The film focuses particularly on the human and environmental consequences of producing cheap clothing on an industrial scale.

Morgan's greatest achievement is making abstract statistics viscerally real. By interweaving interviews with Bangladeshi garment workers, Texan cotton farmers, and industry executives, the documentary resists the temptation of a single villain narrative. Instead, it presents a systemic critique of a model in which the true costs of production — ecological destruction, poverty wages, and compromised worker safety — are externalised onto those least able to bear them.

The footage shot in the aftermath of the Rana Plaza collapse is particularly harrowing and effectively underscores the human stakes involved. Less persuasive, perhaps, are the sections devoted to alternative fashion movements, which occasionally feel underdeveloped compared to the film's devastating central argument.

Nevertheless, 'The True Cost' remains one of the most important environmental documentaries of the past decade. It is required viewing not only for those interested in fashion but for anyone who consumes clothing — which is to say, everyone. Its power lies in demonstrating that consumer choices are never truly neutral.

Highly recommended for secondary school classrooms, university seminars, and anyone with a genuine interest in ethical consumption and global inequality.
